docs.name: Animated Tooltip
docs.description: An accessible, spring-animated tooltip with a customizable pointing arrow. Background, text, and arrow colours are independently configurable, and it follows WAI-ARIA guidance — hover/focus triggers, role="tooltip", aria-describedby, and Escape to dismiss.

props.name: children
props.description: The trigger element. Must be a single focusable React element (e.g. a button or link).

props.name: content
props.description: Content rendered inside the tooltip bubble.

props.name: side
props.description: Which side of the trigger the tooltip appears on.

props.name: background
props.description: Background colour of the tooltip bubble.

props.name: arrowColor
props.description: Arrow fill colour. Defaults to `background` so it always matches.

props.name: arrow
props.description: Whether to render the pointing arrow.

props.name: offset
props.description: Gap between the trigger and the tooltip in px.

props.name: delay
props.description: Delay before the tooltip appears, in ms.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disable the tooltip entirely (the trigger still renders).

props.name: className
props.description: Extra Tailwind classes merged onto the tooltip bubble.

docs.overview: AnimatedTooltip wraps any single focusable element and shows a floating label on hover and keyboard focus. It follows the WAI-ARIA tooltip pattern: the bubble carries role="tooltip", the trigger is linked via aria-describedby while open, and pressing Escape dismisses it. The bubble and its pointing arrow are styled through independent props, so you can recolour the background, the text, and the arrow separately. Enter/exit uses a spring and automatically falls back to a plain fade when the user prefers reduced motion.

scenarios.title: Recolour background, text, and arrow independently
scenarios.description: Set `background`, `color`, and `arrowColor` to fully theme the tooltip. The arrow defaults to the background colour but can be overridden for a contrasting tip.
scenarios.code:
"use client"; import { AnimatedTooltip } from "@/components/ui/animated-tooltip"; export default function ThemedTooltip() { return (
); }

scenarios.title: Placement on any side
scenarios.description: Use `side` and `align` to position the tooltip — the arrow tracks the chosen edge and alignment automatically.

scenarios.title: No arrow with a custom delay
scenarios.description: Set `arrow={false}` for a flat label and tune `delay` to control how quickly it appears.

scenarios.title: Rich, multi-line content
scenarios.description: `content` accepts any React node, so you can render a heading, body copy, or a keyboard shortcut. Use `className` to widen the bubble.

scenarios.title: Any trigger — icon buttons and links
scenarios.description: The trigger can be any single focusable element. Hover and keyboard focus both work because the tooltip listens via bubbling focus events.

accessibility.screenReaderNotes: Bubble uses role="tooltip" and is linked to the trigger via aria-describedby while visible. Shows on hover and keyboard focus, dismisses on Escape and blur. The decorative arrow is aria-hidden.

motion.performanceNotes: Spring scale/translate on enter/exit only; falls back to an opacity-only fade when prefers-reduced-motion is set.

changes: Preserve the trigger's existing aria-describedby when linking the tooltip.
